import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    # Find smallest v >= 0 such that v^n > m
    # Try each bit from high to low: force v^n > m at this bit, minimize remaining bits of v
    best = float('inf')
    # Try making v^n > m by having a 1 at bit b where m has 0, and matching m above bit b
    bits = 30
    for b in range(bits, -1, -1):
        # Check if bit b of m is 0
        if not (m >> b & 1):
            # We want v^n to match m on bits above b, then have 1 at bit b
            # bits above b: (v^n) matches m means v matches (m^n) on those bits
            # at bit b: (v^n) has 1 means v has (1 ^ (n>>b&1)) at bit b
            # bits below b: minimize v, so set to 0
            prefix = (m >> (b+1)) << (b+1)  # m's bits above b
            v = (prefix ^ n) & ~((1<<(b+1))-1)  # v's bits above b to match m^n
            v |= ((1 ^ (n>>b&1)) << b)  # bit b of v
            # bits below b of v = 0
            v = v & ((1<<31)-1)
            if v >= 0 and v^n > m:
                best = min(best, v)
    print(best if best != float('inf') else 0)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
